lessons.title: What Is a Ratio?
lessons.text: A ratio compares two quantities. It tells how much of one thing there is compared to another.
lessons.example: There are 3 cats and 2 dogs. The ratio of cats to dogs is 3 to 2.
lessons.practice_question: Write the ratio of 4 apples to 1 banana.

lessons.title: Writing Ratios in Different Forms
lessons.text: A ratio can be written with 'to', with a colon, or as a fraction.
lessons.practice_question: Write the ratio of 5 red marbles to 3 blue marbles in all three forms.

lessons.title: Equivalent Ratios
lessons.text: Equivalent ratios describe the same relationship. Multiply or divide both parts to create one.
lessons.practice_question: Create an equivalent ratio to 3:5 by multiplying both numbers by 3.

lessons.title: Ratio Tables
lessons.text: A ratio table organizes equivalent ratios.
lessons.example: If 2 sandwiches cost $6, then 4 cost $12 and 6 cost $18.
lessons.practice_question: If 3 notebooks cost $9, how much do 6 notebooks cost?

lessons.title: Unit Rates
lessons.text: A unit rate tells how much happens per 1 unit.
lessons.example: 10 miles in 2 hours → 5 miles per hour.
lessons.practice_question: Find the unit rate: 12 apples cost $6.

lessons.title: What Is a Proportion?
lessons.text: A proportion is when two ratios are equal.

lessons.title: Solving Proportions (Cross-Multiplying)
lessons.text: Cross-multiplying helps find a missing value in a proportion.

lessons.title: Scaling Up and Down
lessons.text: Scaling up multiplies both parts. Scaling down divides both parts.

lessons.title: Percent as a Ratio
lessons.text: A percent is a ratio out of 100.

lessons.title: Converting Percent to Decimal
lessons.text: Divide by 100 to convert a percent to a decimal.

lessons.title: Converting Decimal to Percent
lessons.text: Multiply by 100 to convert a decimal to a percent.

lessons.title: Percent of a Number (Part 1)
lessons.text: Convert the percent to a decimal and multiply.

lessons.title: Percent Problems (Part 3)
lessons.text: Percent increase or decrease: find the percent amount, then add or subtract.
lessons.practice_question: A $40 game increases by 10%. What is the new price?

masteryCheck.question: Write the ratio of 8 pencils to 2 erasers.
Create an equivalent ratio to 4:7 by doubling.
Find the unit rate: 15 miles in 3 hours.

unitTest.question: Write the ratio of 9 oranges to 3 lemons.
